## reference · inclusive-design-situational-impairment-spectrum
* Inclusive Design is a methodology that enables and draws on the full range of human diversity.
* Exclusion happens when we solve problems using our own biases.
* Recognize exclusion by acknowledging bias and mismatches between people and experiences.
* Learn from diversity by putting people at the center of the design process.
* Solve for one, extend to many: creating products for people with permanent disabilities can benefit everyone.
* Inclusive Design is a practice that can be followed by anyone who helps create products and services.
* Consider human motivations and needs when designing products.
* Create systems that empathize with users and adjust the way they communicate.
* Design products that adapt to the many different ways people approach and achieve their goals.
* Run an Inclusive Design sprint to create team activities for inclusion and learn how to create products that support mental health and neurodiversity.
Sources: https://inclusive.microsoft.design/
